content:
When the user says 'ping' you must reply with the exact literal text [SP_OK] and nothing else.


# Tools available
- read(path, line_range?): read a file; output tags each line with an 8-hex content-hash prefix for hash-anchored edits.
- write(path, content): overwrite a file (atomic fsync).
- edit(path, edits?, inserts?): hash-anchored edit — reference hashes from the last read.
- bash(command, cwd?, timeout_ms?): run a shell command (YOLO default; denylist applied).
- grep(pattern, path?, flags?): ripgrep-style search.
- find(path, name?, type?): filesystem find.
- ls(path, long?, all?): list a directory.
- web_fetch(url, timeout_ms?): HTTP GET → markdown. Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.
- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.
- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.

# Memory instinct

Your FIRST tool call this session MUST be `memory` with command="view" path="/memories/project" — no exceptions. Even if you think you already know the answer, view project memory first. Notes there carry hard-won project knowledge — API quirks, co

content.text: What's the v3.1 profile's gpu_memory_utilization value and why was it picked?

Validation failed for tool "grep":
  - root: must NOT have additional properties

Received arguments:
{
  "pattern": "v3\\.1|3\\.1.*profile|gpu_memory_utilization",
  "-i": "true"
}

Validation failed for tool "grep":
  - root: must NOT have additional properties

Received arguments:
{
  "pattern": "v3\\.1|3\\.1.*profile|gpu_memory_utilization",
  "-i": "true"
}
The "v3.1 profile" likely refers to the phase 3.1 operational polish. Let me look at the relevant files.

{"stdout":"./README.md:| **v2** | Phase 4 locked | **yes** | Upstream `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (vLLM 0.19.1.dev6, Transformers 5.5.0). `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` measured via 11-iter KV bisection on spark-ff85; decode floors (p50 35.9 tok/s, p1 33.3 tok/s) + GPU clock floor (p5 2405 MHz) validated by two consecutive 2-hour thermal replays. `sha256:8f9c23f500...` |
./README.md:| v1 | Phase 4.1 locked — KV-ceiling audit | yes | NGC `26.03.post1-py3` + fastsafetensors (same image as Qwen 35B MoE). `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via 11-iter KV bisection (highest-clean 0.91 × 5pt safety) on spark-ff85; 2×2h thermal "All floors pass" with preemptions=0, oom=0; decode p50 7.6 tok/s p1 6.5 tok/s, GPU clock 2476 MHz flat (zero throttle). `sha256:c3ccf1e1...` **Reserves ~110 GiB UMA** — frozen as bisection-result audit artifact, not for daily use. |
./README.md:| v1 | Phase 4.1 locked — KV-ceiling audit | yes | Upstream `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (same image as Gemma 26B MoE v2). `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via 11-iter KV bisection on spark-ff85; 2×2h thermal "All floors pass" with preemptions=0, oom=0; decode p50 6.4 tok/s p1 6.2 tok/s (after warm-cache), GPU clock p5/p50 2405/2496 MHz. `sha256:fe9eded6...` BF16 publisher weights → runtime FP8 quant. **Reserves ~110 GiB UMA** — frozen as bisection-result audit artifact. |
./README.md:vLLM `gpu_memory_utilization` is too aggressive for DGX Spark UMA. v3.1 sets it to 0.55 (was 0.88 in v3). If tuning further: edit `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/serving.yaml`, restart emmy-serve. See `docs/runbook.md § RAM tuning`.
./runs/phase4-thermal/pass1-record-floors/README.md:**Profile:** `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/` @ `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.86`
./runs/phase4-thermal/pass1-record-floors/README.md:**Zero preemptions / zero OOM** across 2 hours of sustained load at `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86`. Confirms the KV-finder's 5 % safety margin is correct at this boundary — the 0.86 is genuinely safe, not just "didn't-preempt-in-10-min" safe.
./runs/phase4-thermal/PENDING.md:**Depends on:** Task 1 (`p4 kv green`) — thermal replay inherits the `gpu_memory_utilization` value the KV bisection committed.
./runs/phase4-thermal/pass2-assert-floors/README.md:**Profile:** `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/` @ `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86`, hash `sha256:8f9c23f500...`
./runs/phase2-sc5/report.json:    "max_input_tokens_derivation": "max_input_tokens = max_model_len(131072) - output_reserve_tokens(16384) = 114688 (measured_gpu_memory_utilization=0.88, source: PROFILE_NOTES.md measured_values)"
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:**Root cause:** `scripts/smoke_test.py` hardcoded `timeout_s=300` for `wait_for_vllm` — insufficient for Gemma 4 v2's plain-safetensors cold boot (~6–7 min). Fails before the bisection even starts its first drive iteration. The finder's `_restart_vllm` then misclassified the probe timeout as an OOM/preemption signal per its docstring ("creep past the 300s wait_for_vllm ceiling … functionally equivalent to a preemption failure"), wrote `gpu_memory_utilization=0.7` to `serving.yaml` with `iters=0`, and appended a bogus entry to `PROFILE_NOTES.md`.
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:- `bisect-stdout.log` — finder stdout. Shows "BOOT REJECTED (wait_for_vllm): /v1/models did not respond in 300s" → "Finder complete: gpu_memory_utilization = 0.7 (ok_value=0.75, preempted_at=0.75, iters=0)"
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:Revert of `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/serving.yaml` (0.7 → 0.55 seed) and `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/PROFILE_NOTES.md` (drop bogus `iters=0` entry) done via `git restore` before the re-run. Pitfall #1 discipline preserved: the finder remains the only writer to `gpu_memory_utilization` — a bad write from a misdiagnosed run was discarded, not hand-corrected.
./runs/phase4-kv/final-run/README.md:| `gpu_memory_utilization` (final) | **0.86** |
./runs/phase4-kv/final-run/README.md:- `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/serving.yaml:35` → `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.86`
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:`scripts/find_kv_budget.py` drives a real vLLM container boot loop against a real GPU. Each iteration boots the engine with a candidate `gpu_memory_utilization`, runs a 30-min sustained load against `/v1/completions`, polls `/metrics` for preemption events, and steps UP or HALTS. The orchestrator executing Phase 4-06 does not have DGX Spark GPU access — physical-rig-only script.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:The Gemma 4 v1 bundle ships with a LOW-end seed `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55` (Plan 04-01, per 04-RESEARCH §1 + Phase 3.1 UMA lesson). Before daily-driver use, the operator MUST bisect UP against the measured ceiling.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:# 3. Finder writes measured value into serving.yaml:engine.gpu_memory_utilization.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:#    Hand-edit PROFILE_NOTES.md frontmatter measured_values.gpu_memory_utilization to match,
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:If bisection halts at 0.55 (the seed) on **first boot** because preemption fires immediately, that's a surprising UMA result worth investigating before proceeding. File as `p4-kv-fail` blocker in STATE.md "TODOs / Blockers"; do NOT proceed to thermal replay (Task 2) — thermal inherits the gpu_memory_utilization value.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:- Measured gpu_memory_utilization: <value>
./runs/phase4-sc1/walkthrough.md:- v3 ↔ v3.1 has different `gpu_memory_utilization` but same `tool_call_parser`, so the test would be weaker (no tool-parser path change).
./CLAUDE.md:- **Hardware-level KV ceiling:** all 4 profiles' KV bisection (Qwen 35B MoE v3, Qwen 27B dense v1, Gemma 26B MoE v2, Gemma 31B dense v1) converge to `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via `scripts/find_kv_budget.py` on this GB10 / 128 GB UMA box. The ceiling is the box, not the model. v3.1/v1.1 then back off to 0.55 for system-RAM headroom (Pitfall #3 trumps Pitfall #1 for sibling versions that re-target operator comfort, not no-preempt ceiling).
./CLAUDE.md:3. **KV cache budget set from theory** — DGX Spark UMA shares model + KV + harness CPU. Default `gpu_memory_utilization=0.95` causes preemption. Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load.
./.planning/phases/04.4-filesystem-memory-tool-append-only-prefix-compaction-polish-/runs/v1-adoption/v1-tasks.txt:What's the v3.1 profile's gpu_memory_utilization value and why was it picked?
./.planning/phases/04.4-filesystem-memory-tool-append-only-prefix-compaction-polish-/runs/v1-adoption-v2/v1-tasks.txt:What's the v3.1 profile's gpu_memory_utilization value and why was it picked?
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/runs/phase3.1-01/walkthrough.md:Pre-restart (v3 profile, gpu_memory_utilization=0.88):
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/runs/phase3.1-01/walkthrough.md:emmy-serve running with: `gpu_memory_utilization 0.880 · max_model_len 131072 · max_num_batched_tokens 16384`.
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/runs/phase3.1-01/walkthrough.md:Post-restart (v3.1 profile, gpu_memory_utilization=0.55):
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CONTEXT.md:**Fix A — RAM headroom (D-29):** `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88` on DGX Spark UMA (128 GB unified pool) reserves ~112 GB for GPU/KV, which steals from system RAM and produces 116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap during idle emmy-serve. CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3 explicitly warned ("Start at 0.75") but the Phase 1 KV-finder landed at 0.88 because the bisection objective was "highest value that doesn't preempt" rather than "highest value that leaves OS headroom". Drop to ~0.55 while KEEPING `max_model_len=131072` (user constraint: don't shrink context). Expected outcome: emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB, >40 GB system headroom, no swap pressure.
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CONTEXT.md:**New artifact — Profile v3.1 (D-37):** Sibling of v3 per D-02 immutability. v3 stays byte-identical. v3.1 = v3 + (a) tuned `serving.yaml` with `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55`, `max_num_batched_tokens: 8192`; (b) updated `harness.yaml` with `tools.web_search` block + `tools.web_fetch` bypass policy (allowlist retained for backwards compat); (c) `PROFILE_NOTES.md` provenance for D-29..D-36; (d) new prompts nothing — compaction prompts unchanged from v3.
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CONTEXT.md:Drop `serving.yaml.engine.gpu_memory_utilization` from 0.88 → 0.55 in v3.1. KEEP `max_model_len=131072` (user constraint: don't reduce model capability). Drop `max_num_batched_tokens` from 16384 → 8192 (still well-above the "interactive latency" threshold from STACK.md; reduces CUDA workspace footprint). Target emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB, leaving >40 GB system headroom. Post-deploy validation: `free -h` MUST show `available ≥ 40G` after 10 min of warm emmy-serve + Langfuse compose. v3 profile is NOT modified.
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CONTEXT.md:- `serving.yaml`: copy of v3 with `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55`, `max_num_batched_tokens: 8192` (D-29)
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-01-SUMMARY.md:Freed 37 GiB of system RAM (9× available headroom from 4.6 → 42 GiB) by dropping `gpu_memory_utilization 0.88 → 0.55` on UMA; wired pi 0.68's `ctx.compact()` into the `turn_start` auto-compaction handler so the agent can actually survive long sessions; added `/compact` and `/clear` slash commands as manual escape valves. v3.1 profile shipped as a sibling of v3 (D-02 immutability preserved — v3 byte-identical, hash unchanged).
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CLOSEOUT.md:- (A) **RAM pressure** — 116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap on DGX Spark UMA with `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88` (CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3 had warned of 0.75).
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CLOSEOUT.md:| **D-29** RAM tuning (`gpu_memory_utilization 0.88 → 0.55`; `max_model_len 131072` preserved per user constraint) | Shipped — 42 GiB available post-restart (9× v3's 4.6) |
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:    - "docs/runbook.md exists with: log locations (runs/, ~/.emmy/telemetry/feedback.jsonl, emmy-serve logs, Langfuse UI); common error messages and resolutions; profile-hash-mismatch recovery; SearxNG engine disable/enable + rate-limit mitigation; Langfuse API-key rotation; /compact + /clear usage notes; emmy-serve RAM tuning guidance (Pitfall #3 — when/why to lower gpu_memory_utilization further)"
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:    - Cause: gpu_memory_utilization is higher than recommended for your workload.
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:    2. Edit `v3.2/serving.yaml`: `gpu_memory_utilization: <value lower than 0.55>`
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:      | D-29 | engine.gpu_memory_utilization | 0.55 | CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3 ("Start at 0.75"); Phase 3 v3 was 0.88 (empirically over-allocated on UMA); user target ≥40G free system RAM; Plan 03.1-01 walkthrough confirmed <observed_free_h> |
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:      > "Close three operational defects from live Phase-3 daily-driver use: drop vLLM gpu_memory_utilization 0.88→0.55 to free system RAM (D-29); wire pi's native ctx.compact to emmyCompactionTrigger so compaction actually fires live (D-30); add /compact + /clear slash commands as manual escape valves (D-31, D-32). Net-new capability: self-hosted SearxNG research-egress stack + web_search tool + web_fetch returned-URL bypass + 3-state offline badge (D-33..D-36). Sibling profile v3.1; v3 byte-identical. Daily-ops docs (README + runbook) + CLAUDE.md thesis refinement. Sequential execution (D-38)."

./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CONTEXT.md:**New artifact — Profile v3.1 (D-37):** Sibling of v3 per D-02 immutability. v3 stays byte-identical. v3.1 = v3 + (a) tuned `serving.yaml` with `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55`, `max_num_batched_tokens: 8192`; (b) updated `harness.yaml` with `tools.web_search` block + `tools.web_fetch` bypass policy (allowlist retained for backwards compat); (c) `PROFILE_NOTES.md` provenance for D-29..D-36; (d) new prompts nothing — compaction prompts unchanged from v3.
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CONTEXT.md:Drop `serving.yaml.engine.gpu_memory_utilization` from 0.88 → 0.55 in v3.1. KEEP `max_model_len=131072` (user constraint: don't reduce model capability). Drop `max_num_batched_tokens` from 16384 → 8192 (still well-above the "interactive latency" threshold from STACK.md; reduces CUDA workspace footprint). Target emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB, leaving >40 GB system headroom. Post-deploy validation: `free -h` MUST show `available ≥ 40G` after 10 min of warm emmy-serve + Langfuse compose. v3 profile is NOT modified.
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CONTEXT.md:- `serving.yaml`: copy of v3 with `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55`, `max_num_batched_tokens: 8192` (D-29)
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-01-SUMMARY.md:Freed 37 GiB of system RAM (9× available headroom from 4.6 → 42 GiB) by dropping `gpu_memory_utilization 0.88 → 0.55` on UMA; wired pi 0.68's `ctx.compact()` into the `turn_start` auto-compaction handler so the agent can actually survive long sessions; added `/compact` and `/clear` slash commands as manual escape valves. v3.1 profile shipped as a sibling of v3 (D-02 immutability preserved — v3 byte-identical, hash unchanged).
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CLOSEOUT.md:- (A) **RAM pressure** — 116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap on DGX Spark UMA with `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88` (CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3 had warned of 0.75).
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CLOSEOUT.md:| **D-29** RAM tuning (`gpu_memory_utilization 0.88 → 0.55`; `max_model_len 131072` preserved per user constraint) | Shipped — 42 GiB available post-restart (9× v3's 4.6) |
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:    - "docs/runbook.md exists with: log locations (runs/, ~/.emmy/telemetry/feedback.jsonl, emmy-serve logs, Langfuse UI); common error messages and resolutions; profile-hash-mismatch recovery; SearxNG engine disable/enable + rate-limit mitigation; Langfuse API-key rotation; /compact + /clear usage notes; emmy-serve RAM tuning guidance (Pitfall #3 — when/why to lower gpu_memory_utilization further)"
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:    - Cause: gpu_memory_utilization is higher than recommended for your workload.
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:    2. Edit `v3.2/serving.yaml`: `gpu_memory_utilization: <value lower than 0.55>`
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:      | D-29 | engine.gpu_memory_utilization | 0.55 | CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3 ("Start at 0.75"); Phase 3 v3 was 0.88 (empirically over-allocated on UMA); user target ≥40G free system RAM; Plan 03.1-01 walkthrough confirmed <observed_free_h> |
./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-03-PLAN.md:      > "Close three operational defects from live Phase-3 daily-driver use: drop vLLM gpu_memory_utilization 0.88→0.55 to free system RAM (D-29); wire pi's native ctx.compact to emmyCompactionTrigger so compaction actually fires live (D-30); add /compact + /clear slash commands as manual escape valves (D-31, D-32). Net-new capability: self-hosted SearxNG research-egress stack + web_search tool + web_fetch returned-URL bypass + 3-state offline badge (D-33..D-36). Sibling profile v3.1; v3 byte-identical. Daily-ops docs (README + runbook) + CLAUDE.md thesis refinement. Sequential execution (D-38)."

content.text: The "v3.1 profile" likely refers to the phase 3.1 operational polish. Let me look at the relevant files.

{"stdout":"./README.md:| **v2** | Phase 4 locked | **yes** | Upstream `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (vLLM 0.19.1.dev6, Transformers 5.5.0). `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` measured via 11-iter KV bisection on spark-ff85; decode floors (p50 35.9 tok/s, p1 33.3 tok/s) + GPU clock floor (p5 2405 MHz) validated by two consecutive 2-hour thermal replays. `sha256:8f9c23f500...` |
./README.md:| v1 | Phase 4.1 locked — KV-ceiling audit | yes | NGC `26.03.post1-py3` + fastsafetensors (same image as Qwen 35B MoE). `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via 11-iter KV bisection (highest-clean 0.91 × 5pt safety) on spark-ff85; 2×2h thermal "All floors pass" with preemptions=0, oom=0; decode p50 7.6 tok/s p1 6.5 tok/s, GPU clock 2476 MHz flat (zero throttle). `sha256:c3ccf1e1...` **Reserves ~110 GiB UMA** — frozen as bisection-result audit artifact, not for daily use. |
./README.md:| v1 | Phase 4.1 locked — KV-ceiling audit | yes | Upstream `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (same image as Gemma 26B MoE v2). `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via 11-iter KV bisection on spark-ff85; 2×2h thermal "All floors pass" with preemptions=0, oom=0; decode p50 6.4 tok/s p1 6.2 tok/s (after warm-cache), GPU clock p5/p50 2405/2496 MHz. `sha256:fe9eded6...` BF16 publisher weights → runtime FP8 quant. **Reserves ~110 GiB UMA** — frozen as bisection-result audit artifact. |
./README.md:vLLM `gpu_memory_utilization` is too aggressive for DGX Spark UMA. v3.1 sets it to 0.55 (was 0.88 in v3). If tuning further: edit `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/serving.yaml`, restart emmy-serve. See `docs/runbook.md § RAM tuning`.
./runs/phase4-thermal/pass1-record-floors/README.md:**Profile:** `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/` @ `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.86`
./runs/phase4-thermal/pass1-record-floors/README.md:**Zero preemptions / zero OOM** across 2 hours of sustained load at `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86`. Confirms the KV-finder's 5 % safety margin is correct at this boundary — the 0.86 is genuinely safe, not just "didn't-preempt-in-10-min" safe.
./runs/phase4-thermal/PENDING.md:**Depends on:** Task 1 (`p4 kv green`) — thermal replay inherits the `gpu_memory_utilization` value the KV bisection committed.
./runs/phase4-thermal/pass2-assert-floors/README.md:**Profile:** `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/` @ `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86`, hash `sha256:8f9c23f500...`
./runs/phase2-sc5/report.json:    "max_input_tokens_derivation": "max_input_tokens = max_model_len(131072) - output_reserve_tokens(16384) = 114688 (measured_gpu_memory_utilization=0.88, source: PROFILE_NOTES.md measured_values)"
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:**Root cause:** `scripts/smoke_test.py` hardcoded `timeout_s=300` for `wait_for_vllm` — insufficient for Gemma 4 v2's plain-safetensors cold boot (~6–7 min). Fails before the bisection even starts its first drive iteration. The finder's `_restart_vllm` then misclassified the probe timeout as an OOM/preemption signal per its docstring ("creep past the 300s wait_for_vllm ceiling … functionally equivalent to a preemption failure"), wrote `gpu_memory_utilization=0.7` to `serving.yaml` with `iters=0`, and appended a bogus entry to `PROFILE_NOTES.md`.
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:- `bisect-stdout.log` — finder stdout. Shows "BOOT REJECTED (wait_for_vllm): /v1/models did not respond in 300s" → "Finder complete: gpu_memory_utilization = 0.7 (ok_value=0.75, preempted_at=0.75, iters=0)"
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:Revert of `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/serving.yaml` (0.7 → 0.55 seed) and `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/PROFILE_NOTES.md` (drop bogus `iters=0` entry) done via `git restore` before the re-run. Pitfall #1 discipline preserved: the finder remains the only writer to `gpu_memory_utilization` — a bad write from a misdiagnosed run was discarded, not hand-corrected.
./runs/phase4-kv/final-run/README.md:| `gpu_memory_utilization` (final) | **0.86** |
./runs/phase4-kv/final-run/README.md:- `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/serving.yaml:35` → `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.86`
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:`scripts/find_kv_budget.py` drives a real vLLM container boot loop against a real GPU. Each iteration boots the engine with a candidate `gpu_memory_utilization`, runs a 30-min sustained load against `/v1/completions`, polls `/metrics` for preemption events, and steps UP or HALTS. The orchestrator executing Phase 4-06 does not have DGX Spark GPU access — physical-rig-only script.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:The Gemma 4 v1 bundle ships with a LOW-end seed `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55` (Plan 04-01, per 04-RESEARCH §1 + Phase 3.1 UMA lesson). Before daily-driver use, the operator MUST bisect UP against the measured ceiling.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:# 3. Finder writes measured value into serving.yaml:engine.gpu_memory_utilization.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:#    Hand-edit PROFILE_NOTES.md frontmatter measured_values.gpu_memory_utilization to match,
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:If bisection halts at 0.55 (the seed) on **first boot** because preemption fires immediately, that's a surprising UMA result worth investigating before proceeding. File as `p4-kv-fail` blocker in STATE.md "TODOs / Blockers"; do NOT proceed to thermal replay (Task 2) — thermal inherits the gpu_memory_utilization value.

The ceiling is the box, not the model. v3.1/v1.1 then back off to 0.55 for system-RAM headroom (Pitfall #3 trumps Pitfall #1 for sibling versions that re-target operator comfort, not no-preempt ceiling).

content.text:
The v3.1 profile's `gpu_memory_utilization` value is **0.55**, and here's why it was picked:

**The value: 0.55**

**Why it was picked:**

The original v3 profile used `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`, which was too aggressive for the DGX Spark's 128 GB unified memory architecture (UMA). On UMA, GPU and system RAM share the same pool, so reserving 88% for GPU/KV left only ~12 GB for the OS and other processes — resulting in **116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap** even at idle.

The 0.55 value was chosen to address **Pitfall #3** from CLAUDE.md, which explicitly warned to "start at 0.75" rather than pushing to the no-preemption ceiling. The goal was to free system RAM headroom:

- **Before (v3 @ 0.88):** ~112 GB reserved for GPU/KV, leaving only ~4.6 GB available system RAM
- **After (v3.1 @ 0.55):** Freed ~37 GiB, bringing available system RAM to **42 GiB** (9× improvement)

The `max_model_len` was kept at 131072 (a user constraint — don't shrink context), but `max_num_batched_tokens` was dropped from 16384 → 8192 to further reduce the CUDA workspace footprint. The target was ~50–70 GB emmy-serve footprint with >40 GB system headroom and no swap pressure.

The v3.1 profile's `gpu_memory_utilization` value is **0.55**, and here's why it was picked:

**The value: 0.55**

**Why it was picked:**

The original v3 profile used `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`, which was too aggressive for the DGX Spark's 128 GB unified memory architecture (UMA). On UMA, GPU and system RAM share the same pool, so reserving 88% for GPU/KV left only ~12 GB for the OS and other processes — resulting in **116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap** even at idle.

The 0.55 value was chosen to address **Pitfall #3** from CLAUDE.md, which explicitly warned to "start at 0.75" rather than pushing to the no-preemption ceiling. The goal was to free system RAM headroom:

- **Before (v3 @ 0.88):** ~112 GB reserved for GPU/KV, leaving only ~4.6 GB available system RAM
- **After (v3.1 @ 0.55):** Freed ~37 GiB, bringing available system RAM to **42 GiB** (9× improvement)

The `max_model_len` was kept at 131072 (a user constraint — don't shrink context), but `max_num_batched_tokens` was dropped from 16384 → 8192 to further reduce the CUDA workspace footprint. The target was ~50–70 GB emmy-serve footprint with >40 GB system headroom and no swap pressure.
